fundamentals.name: Lumentum Holdings Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Lumentum Holdings Inc. manufactures and sells optical and photonic products in the Americas, the Asia-Pacific, Europe, the Middle East, and Africa. It operates through two segments, Cloud & Networking and Industrial Tech. The Cloud & Networking segment offers optical and photonic chips, components, modules, and subsystems supplied to cloud data center operators, AI/ML infrastructure providers, and network equipment manufacturer customers that are building cloud data centers and network infrastructures. Its Industrial Tech segment provides short-pulse solid-state lasers, kilowatt-class fiber lasers, diode lasers, and gas lasers that serves the semiconductor device, solar cell, display, and electric vehicle and battery manufacturing markets. The company was incorporated in 2015 and is headquartered in San Jose, California.

geographic.name: Other Asia Pacific Not Individually Identified

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing company with strong margins and analyst backing, though valuation is high and debt is notable.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat estimates; next earnings date was 2026-05-05.
positives: Revenue growth 90.1% YoY, significantly outpacing estimates.
Operating margin 21.8% and profit margin 17.7% indicate strong profitability.
Return on equity at 22.8% shows efficient use of shareholder capital.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $1104.68, suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 161.42 and Forward P/E of 49.85 indicate a high valuation.
Debt/Equity ratio of 111.44 suggests significant leverage.
Free cash flow of $93.35M appears low relative to market cap and debt.
Purchase obligations are reportedly exploding, a potential concern.

news.paragraph: Lumentum's purchase obligations are reportedly exploding, a development that has prompted rating upgrades and discussions around AI deals and new valuation calls. While some analysts are bullish on the stock's momentum and AI-driven growth, others flag this increase in obligations as a potential red flag. The company's last reported quarter saw an EPS beat, but the next earnings date is May 5th, which is in the past relative to today's date.
